comment.body:
The problem is that some clowns are hammering that buy and sell button without the faintest clue how the products they’re trading actually work or get priced. No tactics, no plan, just chaos. When Alma says “risk half the profit,” that’s risk management talk, not a hard number, because we don’t know her yesterday’s exact gains or how many contracts went into each leg.
So seriously, read first. All the answers are laid out. Look at how many people get it, how many member actually understand Alma’s message. It’s only these five-year-old rookie numbnuts whining because they’re as clueless as a cowboy in Brooklyn. They don’t even grasp the basics but still wanna play pro. Alma’s not teaching kindergarten here, she made it clear from day one: if you’re in this chat, you’d better already know at least what a stock or a futures contract is. Basics. No hand-holding.

ES rallied 58 points after Alma entered her trade today. She said she was only risking half of yesterday’s profits. Just keeping things honest.

comment.body:
Let me tell you something… buckaroo… when she stipulates the size of both trades are 2 legs… when both trades occur in a high risk environment that requires caution, then why would a subscriber assume the size of the “legs” are different if Alma doesn’t disclose so? So if the variable of the position sizing is the same, then you can absolutely compare the two trades based off of ES points. Especially when the only trade that occurred within the “trading signals” thread yesterday is exactly how I described it. If she risked half of the potential max profit yesterday, it’s a 24 point stop on the same position sizing. Plain and simple. 

Are you the one who needs to go back to 2nd grade math class?

Instead of you all going crazy having an outrage reaction, maybe just acknowledge that Alma should be clearer and a bit more transparent with the signals tab, or get rid of it.

How is it helpful or educational if she doesn’t disclose any of these details that are pertinent to her trade? Why even mention she’s risking half of yesterday’s profits if it can’t be assumed in the manner in which I have done so? How is that helpful if she’s using two different position sizes for her legs? It isn’t.

The outrage here is ridiculous. And you are not helping Alma with new subscribers acting this way. If I’m a reasonable person coming on this thread for the first time, I might be questioning when Tom Cruise is gonna come out of the shadows to try to convert me to Scientology. Especially when the “Our Lady” comments and “mommy” comments are surging through.

Vinny’s right, “IF THE GLOVE DON’T FIT, YOU MUST AQUIT!!!”

I haven’t escalated the conversation to this point. I’m simply responding and defending myself.

comment.body:
Alright, Sharcus, lemme school you on how trading actually works, kiddo. You buy ANY amount of shares at a given price. This could be one, could be eight, whatever. If the price moves x %, your entire position moves x %. So if you bought at $1 and it shoots up to $2, that’s a 100% gain. Eight shares? You made $8. Two shares? You only made $2. Simple math.
Now, if tomorrow i'd say i’m risking 'half of yesterday’s profit' just as alma did, where i set my stop-loss depends on how many shares i had yesterday. Made $8? I risk $4. Made $2? I risk $1. Capiche? We’re risking profit, not the entry price.

comment.body:
@Vince welcome here.
Do me a favor.
Every insights is highly appreciated, as you can see, many plp share their thoughts here.
There is two things I don't like: 1) AI generated sums, shortcuts, explainations; 2) distracting plp by trying to fade me, or prove me that I'm wrong in any manner, and act like the smartest. 
it is simply unnecessary. As I said on twitter, I'm not 100% right, just as nobody.
But there is difference when someone who actually has knowledge and understanding critizies me, and when somebody is trying to act smarter. Hope you are not here for that.
In order to fade or critizise somebody you first need to actually understand the other's narrative. 

I said, that there is only one rule here: respect. Vice versa. To me, this things is also included.

comment.body:
Volatility is here, and a lot of plp joined this substack who have nothing to do with this content. 
They are looking for somebody who holds their hand bcs they have no clue about whats going on.
I'm not your mommy. You are not children.
We are all adults. We make responsible decisions when we enter a trade, when we decide to trade. We all had to pass a test on the brokerage platform to be able to trade derivatives, so you all are have at least the basic knowledges.

This is not some furu shit, saying: "oh, bought 540 put at 3.04 avg (*edited 108th times), exited at 4.10, made 158974556489% of profit woah"
My basic assumption is that you have trading background, etc. and you came here to actually understand what is actually going on.
I can explain this for you, if you listen.
This is what I'm doing day by day.
I have serious connections, I have infos that you cannot even imagine, and I can see things forward most of the times.
I'm not here to make you addicted, I'm here to make you independent. 
And what I explain here, nobody explains. 
If you listen, you will understand. if you don't ,then not. So simple it is.
